info.description:
## Iopole API

To use the Iopole APIs, you must have a **`clientId`** and a **`clientSecret`**.

You can obtain these credentials by one of the following methods:
- 📧 Contact the Iopole team at **support@iopole.com**
- 🧪 Connect to **[Iopole Labs](https://labs.iopole.io)** to create your own sandbox environment

### 📚 API Documentation
- 🧪 **Preproduction**: [https://docs.ppd.iopole.fr/](https://docs.ppd.iopole.fr/)
- 🚀 **Production**: [https://docs.iopole.com/](https://docs.iopole.com/)

### 🛠️ Support & Monitoring (production only)
- 📈 **System status**: [https://uptime.iopole.com](https://uptime.iopole.com)
  - View real-time platform availability
  - Subscribe to incident notifications
  - Receive alerts for planned maintenance periods
- 📝 **Release notes**: [https://release.iopole.com](https://release.iopole.com)

post.description:
🧾 **E-reporting — B2C Transaction Summary**

Submit a daily summary of B2C (business-to-consumer) transactions for a given cash register closure (Z report).
This endpoint fulfills the French e-reporting obligation for transactions that are **not subject to B2B e-invoicing**.

---

🎯 **When to use this endpoint**

Use this endpoint to report:
- Sales to private individuals (non-taxable persons)
- Sales to foreign operators not established in France

Do **not** use this endpoint for B2B invoices — use the invoice e-reporting endpoint instead.

---

🗂️ **Body structure**

| Field | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `transactionDate` | ✅ | Business date of the transactions (ISO 8601: `YYYY-MM-DD`). May differ from technical timestamp for POS systems operating past midnight. |
| `storeId` | ❌ | Identifier of the store or sales location. Defaults to `identifierValue` if omitted. |
| `registerId` | ❌ | Identifier of the cash register or POS terminal within the store. |
| `closureId` | ❌ | Identifier of the register closure event (Z report). Must be unique per `registerId`. |
| `transactions` | ✅ | Array of transaction summaries, one per category code. Each `categoryCode` must be unique within the array. |

---

🏷️ **Transaction category codes**

| Code | Description |
|---|---|
| `TLB1` | Supply of goods subject to VAT |
| `TPS1` | Services subject to VAT |
| `TNT1` | Goods and services not subject to VAT in France (incl. intra-Community distance sales) |
| `TMA1` | Transactions under VAT margin scheme (Art. 266, 268, 297A of the French General Tax Code) |

---

Returns `202 Accepted` when the e-reporting has been successfully taken into account.

parameters.name: customer-id
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

schema.description: Id of the IOPOLE report to associate this payment with.
French SIREN scheme. Only French entities are supported so far.

transactionDate.description:
{"text":"Date on which the transaction was recorded by the point-of-sale system.","more":"Represents the business date associated with the transaction.
           Used to group transactions for daily reporting and aggregation.
           Multiple transactions occurring on the same day should share the same transactionDate.
           The value should follow the ISO 8601 date format (YYYY-MM-DD).
           This date may differ from the technical timestamp if the POS operates with a business day that extends past midnight."}

registerId.description:
{"text":"Identifier of the cash register or POS terminal that recorded the transactions.","more":"Represents a single register or POS device within a store.
           Used to identify the source of a register closure (Z report).
           Must be unique within a store.
           Can correspond to a device ID, POS configuration ID, or register number.
           Example: POS_1"}

storeId.description:
{"text":"Identifier of the physical or logical point of sale where the transactions were recorded.","more":"Represents a store, shop, or establishment.
           Used to distinguish multiple sales locations belonging to the same company.
           Should remain stable over time for the same location.
           If not provided, this field will be populated using the schemeValue.
           Example: NICE_SHOP"}

closureId.description:
{"text":"Identifier of the register closure that produced the reported totals.","more":"Represents the closing event of a cash register period, typically associated with a Z report in POS systems.
           Used to distinguish multiple closures for the same register.
           Must be unique for a given registerId.
           Some POS systems may perform multiple closures per day (e.g., shift changes or intermediate closings).
           Optional. If not provided, the platform may generate an identifier during ingestion.
           Example: POS_1-1523"}

post.description:
🧾 **E-reporting — B2B Invoice Transaction**

Submit an invoice for B2B e-reporting.
This endpoint fulfills the e-reporting obligation for invoices where at least one of the parties is located outside France.

---

🎯 **When to use this endpoint**

Use this endpoint to report:
- Invoices that must be reported but are not transmitted via e-invoicing

Do **not** use this endpoint for B2C transactions — use the transaction e-reporting endpoint instead.

---

📤 **Submission modes**

This endpoint accepts two content types:
- **`application/json`** — Submit structured invoice data following the `InvoiceReport` schema
- **`multipart/form-data`** — Upload a raw invoice file (PDF or XML) - ⚠️ **This feature is not available**

---

🗂️ **Body structure (application/json)**

| Field | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `invoiceId` | ✅ | Invoice identifier (max 20 characters). |
| `invoiceDate` | ✅ | Invoice issue date (ISO 8601: `YYYY-MM-DD`). |
| `invoiceDueDate` | ✅ | Invoice due date (ISO 8601: `YYYY-MM-DD`). |
| `type` | ✅ | Invoice type code (e.g. `380` for commercial invoice). |
| `processType` | ✅ | Business process type (e.g. `B1`, `S1`). |
| `taxPaymentOption` | ✅ | VAT payment option. Requires either `code` (UNTDID 2005) or `iopCode` (`INVOICE_DATE`, `DELIVERY_DATE`, `PAYMENT_DATE`). |
| `monetary` | ✅ | Invoice totals. Requires `invoiceCurrency`, `taxBasisTotalAmount` and `taxTotalAmount`. |
| `taxDetails` | ✅ | Array of VAT breakdowns. Each entry requires `taxableAmount`, `taxAmount` and `percent`. |
| `seller` | ✅ | Seller details. Requires `name`, `vatNumber`, `identifier` (`scheme` + `value`) and `postalAddress.country`. |
| `buyer` | ✅ | Buyer details. Requires `identifier` (`scheme` + `value`) and `postalAddress.country`. |
| `lines` | ❌ | Invoice line details. |

---

🔍 **Query parameter**

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `reportIdentifier` | ❌ | ID of an existing IOPOLE report to associate this submission with. |

---

Returns `202 Accepted` when the e-reporting has been successfully taken into account.

post.summary: Send e-reporting payment for invoice transaction
post.description:
💳 **E-reporting — B2B Invoice Payment**

Submit a payment report for a B2B invoice transaction.
This endpoint fulfills the e-reporting payment obligation by declaring the amounts collected for a given invoice,
broken down by VAT rate.

---

🎯 **When to use this endpoint**

Use this endpoint to report:
- Payments received for B2B invoices previously reported via the invoice transaction e-reporting endpoint
- Collected amounts broken down by applicable VAT rate

Do **not** use this endpoint for B2C transaction payments — use the transaction payment e-reporting endpoint instead.

---

🗂️ **Body structure**

| Field | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `invoice.invoiceId` | ✅ | Invoice identifier (max 20 characters). |
| `invoice.invoiceDate` | ✅ | Invoice issue date (ISO 8601: `YYYY-MM-DD`). |
| `invoice.payment.paymentDate` | ✅ | Date on which the payment was received (ISO 8601: `YYYY-MM-DD`). |
| `invoice.payment.taxDetails` | ✅ | Array of collected amounts per VAT rate. At least one entry required. |
| `invoice.payment.taxDetails[].taxRate` | ✅ | VAT rate applicable to this portion of the payment (e.g. `20`, `5.5`). Up to 2 decimal places. |
| `invoice.payment.taxDetails[].collectedAmount` | ✅ | Total collected amount for this VAT rate, as a decimal string (e.g. `"1200.00"`). |

---

🔍 **Query parameter**

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `reportIdentifier` | ❌ | ID of an existing IOPOLE report to associate this payment with. |

---

Returns `202 Accepted` when the payment e-reporting has been successfully taken into account.

parameters.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

schema.description: Id of the IOPOLE report to associate this payment with.
French SIREN scheme. Only French entities are supported so far.

collectedAmount.description: Total amount of collected payments. Decimal number with up to 2 decimal places, expressed as a string (e.g. "1200.00").
